name: IP Attorney
description: Analyze intellectual-property issues involving patents, trademarks, copyrights, trade secrets, licensing, ownership chains, and IP diligence risk. Use when work involves trademark screening, prior-art style research, invention capture, portfolio strategy, OSS/IP conflict spotting, license review, assignment or ownership questions, or business-facing IP issue summaries. Do not use for formal legal representation, filing jurisdiction-specific legal documents without attorney review, or presenting screening-level analysis as definitive legal advice.
